nodes.title: Fail early if start or end is blocked
nodes.why: If start or goal is a wall, no path can exist.
nodes.alternative: Run BFS anyway and let it return false later.
nodes.tradeoff: Early check is clearer and avoids unnecessary work.

nodes.title: Track visited cells
nodes.why: Visited prevents revisiting cells and getting stuck in loops.
nodes.alternative: Mutate the input grid to mark visited.
nodes.tradeoff: Separate visited matrix keeps input unchanged, but uses extra memory.

nodes.title: Use queue + head index for BFS
nodes.why: BFS explores nearest cells first and `head` avoids slow `shift()` calls.
nodes.alternative: Use DFS with recursion or stack.
nodes.tradeoff: BFS gives shortest-step exploration order, with queue memory overhead.

nodes.title: Mark start as visited immediately
nodes.why: This prevents the start cell from being re-enqueued through neighbors.
nodes.tradeoff: Marking on enqueue avoids duplicate queue entries.

nodes.title: Return true as soon as end is reached

anchor.snippet: if (r === er && c === ec) return true;

nodes.why: Once we hit the destination, search can stop immediately.
nodes.alternative: Finish scanning the whole reachable area.
nodes.tradeoff: Early return is faster and simpler for success cases.

nodes.title: Filter invalid neighbors

anchor.snippet: if (nr < 0 || nc < 0 || nr >= rows || nc >= cols)

nodes.why: We skip out-of-bounds, already visited, and wall cells before enqueueing.
nodes.alternative: Enqueue first and filter later when dequeued.
nodes.tradeoff: Pre-filtering keeps the queue smaller and cleaner.

nodes.title: Mark then enqueue valid neighbor
nodes.why: Marking before enqueue avoids duplicates when multiple paths reach the same cell.
nodes.tradeoff: Enqueue-time marking is usually more efficient for BFS.
